_comment: Illustrative output. Actual LLM output will vary.

battlecard.headline: We win when buyers need reliable integrations and transparent pricing; CompetitorX wins on price optics and AI hype — both are defeatable with the right discovery.

when_we_win.situation: Prospect has a complex tech stack with 10+ tools
when_we_win.reason: Our 200+ native connectors and API quality eliminate the integration risk CompetitorX creates with their smaller marketplace

when_we_win.situation: Security and compliance are in the buying criteria
when_we_win.reason: We are SOC 2 Type II certified; CompetitorX is not, which fails most enterprise security reviews

when_we_win.situation: Buyer has been burned by slow vendor support
when_we_win.reason: Our 3-day onboarding and SLA-backed support are proven differentiators against CompetitorX's 14-day average

when_we_lose.reason: We have no native mobile app until Q3 2026; CompetitorX has full iOS/Android

when_we_lose.situation: Budget is fixed and below our entry price
when_we_lose.reason: CompetitorX's 20% annual discount makes them cheaper in year 1 for deals under $500/mo

when_we_lose.situation: Prospect is deeply embedded in CompetitorX's ecosystem
when_we_lose.reason: Switching cost argument is real if they use CompetitorX's marketplace integrations we do not have

objection_handling.objection: Their price is lower than yours
objection_handling.response: CompetitorX's list price is lower, but their true cost includes per-seat add-ons for reporting, SSO, and API access that push enterprise TCO 30-40% higher than their headline price. Let me show you a side-by-side of what you would actually pay for equivalent capabilities.
objection_handling.avoid_saying: Our pricing reflects our value — that is not an argument, it is a deflection.

objection_handling.objection: They have AI features we do not see in your product
objection_handling.response: Their AI assistant is currently in beta and limited to Enterprise plan users. We would want to understand what specific AI outcome you are trying to achieve — our roadmap includes [X] which addresses the same problem. Can you walk me through the workflow you are hoping AI will improve?
objection_handling.avoid_saying: AI is overrated — that dismisses their legitimate interest.

objection_handling.objection: We have been using them for two years — switching cost is high
objection_handling.response: That is a fair concern. Our migration team has moved 40+ teams from CompetitorX in the last 12 months. We do it in 3 days on average. Can we schedule a technical call to scope the actual migration complexity for your setup?
objection_handling.avoid_saying: Migration is easy — it minimises a real concern.

discovery_questions: How many tools does your team currently connect to your project management system?
Have you gone through a security or compliance review for software in the last year — and what did it require?
What does it cost your team when a project runs late because of a missing integration or a broken sync?
Who on your team reviews the vendor's security certifications before procurement approval?
landmines: Have you seen CompetitorX's SOC 2 Type II certification documentation? (They do not have one — this surfaces the gap without you saying it)
How does your team handle integrations that CompetitorX's marketplace does not cover natively?
What has been your experience with their support response time on production issues?

claims_rebuttal.their_claim: "Cheapest enterprise PM tool on the market"
claims_rebuttal.our_response: This claim is based on base seat price. CompetitorX charges separately for advanced reporting, API access, and SSO — features included in our Business plan. Enterprise TCO is typically comparable.
claims_rebuttal.evidence: Request their full price list including add-ons and compare against our Business plan pricing sheet

claims_rebuttal.our_response: This figure appears in their marketing but no methodology or case study is published. The AI feature is currently in beta on Enterprise plan only.
claims_rebuttal.evidence: Their feature page — beta label is visible; no published case study as of July 2026

claims_rebuttal.their_claim: "Enterprise-grade security by default on all plans"
claims_rebuttal.our_response: Enterprise-grade is undefined in their documentation. They do not hold SOC 2 Type II certification. We do, with a full audit report available under NDA.
claims_rebuttal.evidence: Our SOC 2 Type II report available from sales team; CompetitorX's trust page shows no equivalent certification

battlecard.recent_changes_impact: The Series C and 20% price cut signal CompetitorX is in growth mode and willing to buy market share. Expect more aggressive discounting in competitive deals. Their AI acquisition adds roadmap risk — they may close some of our current differentiation gaps within 12-18 months. In deals closing in the next 6 months, our SOC 2 and integration depth remain durable advantages.
battlecard.confidence_rationale: Objection handling and win/loss scenarios are based on structured input, not primary win/loss interview data. Claims rebuttal evidence should be verified before use with prospects.
